titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sherwin B. Nuland Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 21, 2021 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Sherwin B. Nuland Papers, which span from 1940 to 2014, contain correspondence, writings, audiovisual materials, electronic media, and other papers related to Sherwin B. Nuland's life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 113-120 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 121 (Electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 122 (Patient records): Restricted until 2105. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 123 (Student records): Restricted until 2088. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Correspondence, 1970-2013. II. Writings, 1970-2014. III. Appearance files, 1970-2014. IV. Subject files, 1950-2009. V. Personal papers, 1940-2009. VI. Audiovisual materials, 1980-1999. VII. Digital media, 1996-2013. VIII. Restricted papers, 1966-2005, IX. 2019 acquisition, 2006-2010.

bioghist.head: Sherwin B. Nuland
bioghist.p: Sherwin B. "Shep" Nuland (1930-2014) was a surgeon, educator, and author. Born in the Bronx, New York, he received his bachelor's degree from New York University in 1951 and went on to study medicine at Yale University. Nuland graduated from Yale in 1955 and worked as a surgeon at Yale-New Haven hospital from 1962-1992. Nuland was also a clinical professor at Yale, teaching from 1962-1991. Over the course of his career, Dr. Nuland researched the history of medicine and the cultural history of death and suffering. Nuland's research produced numerous magazine articles for publications including" The New Republic," as well as several books including "Doctors: The Biography of Medicine" (1988), "The Uncertain Art" (2008), and his most well-known work, "How We Die," which won the National Book Award in 1994 and was a finalist for the Pulitzer Prize in nonfiction in 1995. Dr. Nuland died of prostate cancer.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sherwin B. Nuland Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, correspondence, photographs, research files, family papers, patient records, awards, and other papers by or related to Sherwin Nuland.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sherwin B. Nuland Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Letters regarding How We Die, A-M
Letters regarding How We Die, N-Z

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes galley edited draft of "The Doctors Plague."

AAHM, JHM, Beaumont Club, Fulton Fellow
AAHM, JHM, Beaumont Club, Fulton Fellow
How We Die foreign correspondence and World Economic Forum
Leonardo Da Vinci, The Plague of Doctors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, research, and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, research, and related correspondence.

did.unittitle: How We Die typescripts, proofs, and clippings
How We Die correspondence and corrected proofs
Final manuscript and galleys for "How We Die," with heart illustration
Notes and articles for "How We Die"
Notes and articles for "How We Die"
Clippings, mostly related to How We Die
Wisdom of the Body, galleys with edits
Writings and notes - Wisdom of the Body
Wisdom of the Body, cover proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes draftes, notebooks, and other materials.

did.unittitle: Articles and galleys for "The Mysteries Within"
Articles and galleys for "The Mysteries Within"
Correspondence and drafts relating to Doctors
Correspondence and drafts relating to Doctors
Notes and articles for "Finding My Name" and "Lost in America"
Notes and articles for "Lost in America"
Face of Mercy drafts and research
Articles, notes, and manuscripts for Hodgkin and Semmelweis
Appearances, including How We Die paperback tour
Talks, appearances, book tour, and engagements that were turned down
Notes and correspondence related to appearances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies, print outs, and clippings of journal and magazine articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies, print outs, and clippings of journal and magazine articles.

did.unittitle: Ethics and the Humanities in Medicine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subjects include the history of medicine, medical education, the history of science, writing, philosophy, biology, malpractice, infanticide, military medicine, dentistry, Judaica, art, and music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subjects include the history of medicine, medical education, the history of science, writing, philosophy, biology, malpractice, infanticide, military medicine, dentistry, Judaica, art, and music.

did.unittitle: End of life, Yale, CT medicine
Publications - New Republic and New Yorker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a draft of End of Life.

did.unittitle: Subject files - Wisdom of the Body

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopied research articles for The Face of Mercy .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous articles by Nuland, reviews of Prozac and How We Die, Nuland's curriculum vitae, and portrait photographs of Nuland.

did.unittitle: National academies, Yale course, De Bakey
Library, healthcare reform, animal rights information
New England surgical society, HSR, hospice, imbroglio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files relating to winning the National Book Award, includes memorabilia, correspondence and other materiales.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain the organization present at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains materials including writings, clippings, research notes, and correspondence along with documentation related to Nuland's "Canterbury Tales" project.

did.unittitle: Writings, clippings, research notes, and correspondence
